message:
perf(pager,wal): fuse frame assembly into single-pass to eliminate intermediate allocations

bd-db300.3.8.6: In the pager's consolidated-batch write path, replace
the two-pass flat_map + filter/max pattern with a single iteration that
builds frame_refs and computes final_db_size simultaneously, removing the
intermediate `all_frames` Vec allocation.

In the WAL writer, fuse frame construction and checksum computation into
one loop, eliminating the `Vec<WalChecksumTransform>` allocation and the
redundant second `write_wal_frame_salts` call that
`finalize_prepared_frame_bytes` performed. The fused path calls
`append_finalized_prepared_frame_bytes` directly with the running
checksum and last-commit offset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
